symbols: enthroned Christ at center — the Pantocrator, ruler of all creation
open Gospel book — Christ as the Word and Teacher of truth
right hand raised in blessing — the divine blessing and authority
gold background — the uncreated light of divine glory
four living creatures — the symbols of the four Evangelists (lion, ox, eagle, man)
apostles in side panels — the twelve who received and transmitted the Gospel
description: A large Byzantine-style icon on a gold background. At center, a large figure of Christ sits enthroned, wearing a blue robe over a golden tunic. He holds an open red book in his left hand and raises his right hand in a gesture of blessing. A cruciform halo surrounds his head. Around him, winged creatures (the symbols of the four Evangelists) support the throne. Along the left and right borders, small bust-length figures of individual apostles are arranged in vertical rows, each with a halo and holding books or scrolls. Additional small figures appear at the bottom center.
theological_description: This icon depicts Christ in Majesty — the Pantocrator ('Ruler of All') — enthroned in glory surrounded by the apostles. The composition draws on Revelation 4-5, where the One seated on the throne is surrounded by four living creatures and the twenty-four elders. The four winged beings — traditionally identified as the symbols of the Evangelists (Matthew as man, Mark as lion, Luke as ox, John as eagle) — derive from Ezekiel 1:10 and Revelation 4:7. Christ holds the Gospel open, fulfilling His identity as the Logos, the Word of God (John 1:1). His right hand raised in blessing echoes the priestly benediction of Numbers 6:24-26. The apostles arrayed along the borders represent the foundation of the Church: 'built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the cornerstone' (Eph 2:20). The gold background represents the uncreated divine light — 'God is light, and in him is no darkness at all' (1 John 1:5). This is an eschatological image of Christ's final glory, when He will come 'seated at the right hand of Power' (Mark 14:62).

scripture_refs.reason: The One seated on the throne surrounded by four living creatures
In the beginning was the Word — Christ holding the Gospel book
The four faces of the living creatures — lion, ox, man, eagle
Built on the foundation of the apostles — the apostles flanking Christ
You will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power
